SK Hynix plans to raise $30 billion in a US listing to meet memory chip demand. Oil extends decline amid ongoing US-Iran talks. Max Kettner of HSBC sees an expiry date to US exceptionalism in the near term. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tech Stocks Set to Bounce After $1.3 Trillion Rout on AI Jitters

title: European Stocks Steady Ahead of Micron Earnings  | The Opening Trade 6/24/2026
title_cn: 欧洲股市稳定领先美光科技财报|开盘交易 2026 年 6 月 24 日
summary:
Equity markets steadied after a brief selloff in top-performing technology names, with Micron’s earnings taking on added significance as a test for the rally in artificial intelligence stocks. Micron’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: UK bank stocks are well placed to catch up to the loftier valuations of European peers in coming years as they benefit from above-par growth in profitability and stronger capital generation, according to Berenberg analysts.

summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Joyce Chang, JPMorgan Global Research Chair; Clemens Fuest, Ifo Institute President; Miljan Gutovic, Holcim CEO; Benoit Bazin, Saint-Gobain CEO.    (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investor Who Scored 900% Win in 2008 Crisis Has New Big Short Bet

summary: Options traders are building up bets that the broader market overreacted to the Federal Reserve’s hawkish pivot when it priced in a series of interest-rate hikes in the US starting later this year.

summary: Rheinmetall AG shares plunged Wednesday as Germany withdrew from a key warships contract, dealing a blow to investor confidence in the defense contractor following years of huge gains.

summary: Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC, says investors may want to be “perhaps really leaning a bit more into that European story,” as he sees an August expiration date on US exceptionalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: The cost of diesel in the US fell below $5 a gallon for the first time since mid-March, offering some relief for one of the global economy’s most important fuels.

summary: India’s benchmark bond yields and swap rates fell to their lowest levels in three months as the nation’s central bank Governor pushed back against expectations for tighter monetary policy.

summary: Some Indian state-run financial institutions are planning to raise overseas loans of as much as $2 billion using a central bank facility for borrowing at concessional rates, according to people familiar with the matter.

summary: The volatility in South Korea’s benchmark equity index has become so extreme that investors and analysts are likening the market’s surging intra-day swings to the meme-stock frenzy.

summary: MSCI Inc. warned Turkey that it may review its index classification if the country doesn’t improve shareholder transparency.

summary: Copper and industrial metals largely held Tuesday’s slump as a stronger dollar and the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance weighed on the demand outlook.

summary: One of the world’s biggest operators of supertankers has provisionally booked a vessel to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at nearly nine times benchmark rates, a price that reflects the shortage of avalable ships in the area.

title: CATL Sees Its Sodium Batteries in at Least 10,000 EVs This Year

summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. expects 10,000 to 20,000 electric vehicles to carry its sodium-ion batteries this year, a senior official for the Chinese company said.

title_cn: Cerebras 发布令人失望的 2026 年销售前景后暴跌
summary: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares plunged in early trading after the newly public chipmaker gave an annual sales forecast that disappointed investors who were expecting the company to carve out a bigger slice of the AI data center market.

title: Micron Earnings to Be ‘Gut Check Moment’ for Markets, Dan Ives Says
title_cn: 丹·艾夫斯表示，美光的盈利将成为市场的“直觉检查时刻”
summary: Dan Ives, global head of tech research and senior equity analyst at Wedbush Securities, discusses the importance of Wednesday’s earnings from Micron Technologies, tech spending on capex and the AI buildout, and how he views public-private partnerships on AI companies. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lingyi iTech Raises $1.1 Billion With Shares Priced at Maximum
title_cn: 凌逸科技融资11亿美元，股价最高
summary: China’s Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. raised HK$8.3 billion ($1.1 billion) after pricing its Hong Kong listing at the maximum price, with strong investor demand leading the company to reject more than 100 orders, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Fight Against Child Predators Gets Short Shrift While Cases Explode in AI Era

title: Waymo Charging Partner Terawatt Raises Up to $300 Million in Debt
title_cn: Waymo 充电合作伙伴 Terawatt 筹集高达 3 亿美元的债务
summary: Terawatt Infrastructure Inc., a company that provides chargers for electric cars and counts Waymo as a customer, said it is borrowing as much as $300 million from banks to support the acquisition and development of charging facilities in the US and abroad.

title: Meta, Microsoft Lead $850 Billion Boom in Data Center Leases
title_cn: Meta 和微软引领数据中心租赁价值 8500 亿美元的热潮
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. and Microsoft Corp. each committed tens of billions of dollars in additional data center leases in their most recent quarters, adding to the massive sums the industry is spending on artificial intelligence.

title: SK Hynix Seeks to Raise $29 Billion in US Listing to Meet AI Chip Demand
title_cn: SK海力士寻求在美国上市筹集290亿美元以满足人工智能芯片需求
summary: SK Hynix Inc. is planning to raise $29.4 billion (45.45 trillion won) in a landmark US listing and expects trading to start on July 10, according to a regulatory filing on Wednesday. The deal, depending on the exchange rate, could be among the top five share sales of all time. Peter Elstrom reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: MGX Sets Up $50 Billion AI Fund
title_cn: MGX 设立 500 亿美元人工智能基金
summary: Abu Dhabi’s MGX has raised close to $50 billion from regional and global investors, marking one of the biggest ever dedicated AI investment vehicles. Nandu Nair reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI-Powered Emails Are Influencing Politics on Hot-Button Issues
title_cn: 人工智能驱动的电子邮件正在影响热点问题上的政治
summary: From California to North Carolina, officials weighing policy changes are being inundated with comments via platforms that tout AI capabilities.

title: Scammers Are Using AI to Create Fake Auto Loan Documents
title_cn: 骗子利用人工智能创建虚假汽车贷款文件
summary: It’s yet another headache for US auto lenders facing the highest delinquency rates in years

title: AI Billionaires Score Win in $29 Million New York Proxy Fight
title_cn: AI 亿万富翁在纽约 2900 万美元代理权争夺战中获胜
summary: Artificial intelligence billionaires including venture capitalist Marc Andreessen and OpenAI President Greg Brockman scored a key victory in a New York City congressional primary that became a $29 million proxy battle over who will control the future of AI.

title: Fintech Unicorn Is Said to Tap Citi and EFG for Cairo Listing
title_cn: 据称，金融科技独角兽将邀请花旗集团和 EFG 在开罗上市
summary: Fintech MNT-Halan is planning an initial public offering of its Egyptian business in Cairo as soon as this year, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest firm preparing a share sale in the Middle East’s most populous nation.

title: Data Center Buildout Limited by Labor Shortages, Saint-Gobain Says
title_cn: 圣戈班表示数据中心建设受到劳动力短缺的限制
summary: Saint-Gobain CEO Benoit Bazin tells Bloomberg Television that labor bottlenecks are limiting data center build-out in North America, a development that’s coming to Europe. Saint-Gobain manufactures glass products and construction materials. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: German Rail Services Disrupted After Comms System Failure
title_cn: 通信系统故障后德国铁路服务中断
summary: Germany’s national rail operator is struggling to restore regular service after a communications system outage apparently caused by a replacement component brought trains to a nationwide standstill late Tuesday.

title: Zhipu Said to Weigh Multibillion-Dollar Share Sale in Hong Kong After 2,000% Gain
title_cn: 智普股价上涨 2000% 后称考虑在香港出售数十亿美元的股票
summary: Chinese artificial-intelligence model maker Zhipu is considering a share sale to raise several billion US dollars in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said, after soaring about 2,000% since its listing in January.

title: JPMorgan's Chang Says AI Capex Story 'Remains Intact'
title_cn: 摩根大通的张表示人工智能资本支出故事“保持不变”
summary: JPMorgan Global Research Chair Joyce Chang discusses the outlook for technology stocks, explaining why she thinks the AI capex-beneficiary story "remains one that's still very much intact." Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Chang says retail flows indicate "there is still a sentiment to buy the dips." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Philippine Tycoon Boosts PhilWeb Investment in Online Gaming Push
title_cn: 菲律宾大亨加大 PhilWeb 在线游戏投资
summary: Philippine tycoon Lance Gokongwei is acquiring a 15% stake in online gaming technology firm PhilWeb Corp., boosting the company’s foray into artificial intelligence and the billionaire’s exposure to the gaming industry.

title: HSBC Wealth Survey Shows AI Losing Out to Humans in Key Areas
title_cn: 汇丰银行财富调查显示人工智能在关键领域输给了人类
summary: For the truly rich, artificial intelligence can’t compete with human advisers in handling many of their needs, according to a report from HSBC Holdings Plc.

title: UBS' Fowler Sees Value in US Cyclicals, Pharmaceuticals
title_cn: 瑞银集团的福勒看到了美国周期股和制药业的价值
summary: UBS Chief European Equity Strategist and Head of Derivates Gerry Fowler discusses the factors behind the tech selloff and where he sees value in markets. "You're going to get volatility — this isn't the first time we've seen this," Fowler tells Bloomberg Television. "It's an artifice of market structure that I suspect is not going away." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chip Startup Backed by Ex-ASML CEO Seeks to Raise €200 Million
title_cn: 由前 ASML 首席执行官支持的芯片初创公司寻求筹集 2 亿欧元
summary: Euclyd, a Dutch chip startup backed by former ASML Holding NV Chief Executive Officer Peter Wennink, is seeking to raise about €200 million ($229 million) in Series A financing.

title: Masayoshi Son Calls AI Bubble Talk an ‘Insult,’ Delays Retiring
title_cn: 孙正义称人工智能泡沫谈话是“侮辱”并推迟退休
summary: SoftBank Group Corp.’s Masayoshi Son brushed aside concerns about an AI bubble, saying that such a characterization is inappropriate for a revolution that’s only just begun. He vowed to remain at the helm of his investment group much longer to capitalize on the AI boom.

title: Tencent Testing New AI Agent for WeChat Workplace App
title_cn: 腾讯测试微信办公应用的新人工智能代理
summary: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is preparing to launch an AI agent for its Slack-like enterprise communication app, intensifying a high-stakes battle among Chinese tech giants to lock users into their ecosystems in the post-ChatGPT era.

title: Leveraged Korea ETFs Sold Estimated $6 Billion of Shares in Rout
title_cn: 韩国杠杆 ETF 惨遭出售估计价值 60 亿美元的股票
summary: Leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. or SK Hynix Inc. probably sold a combined $6 billion of the Korean chipmakers’ shares Tuesday to maintain their ratios, underscoring such products are amplifying market moves, Bloomberg Intelligence says.

title: CATL's Jun on Sodium-Ion Batteries and AI Technology
title_cn: 宁德时代君谈钠离子电池与AI技术
summary: CATL says it is focusing on deploying sodium-ion batteries this year to meet demand for AI-related energy storage. CATL Chief Manufacturing Officer Ni Jun discusses the battery maker's outlook, as well as the prospect of harnessing computing power from idle electric vehicles. He speaks on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Sells $25 Billion of Bonds, Cuts Interest Costs
title_cn: SpaceX 出售 250 亿美元债券，降低利息成本
summary: SpaceX sold $25 billion of investment-grade bonds on Tuesday, marking the final step to replace the costly debt that had helped finance Elon Musk’s acquisition of X, then known as Twitter, as well as the expensive loans and bonds issued by xAI to bridge its rapid cash drain. Bloomberg's Manuel Baigorri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kioxia’s Executive Pay Jumps After AI Boosts Demand and Stock
title_cn: 人工智能提振需求和库存后，铠侠高管薪酬大幅上涨
summary: Executive pay at Japanese flash memory maker Kioxia Holdings Corp. surged, a reflection of runaway global demand for AI-related chips.

title: Mamdani Clout Grows as NYC Races Show Anti-Establishment Anger
title_cn: 纽约赛马表现出反建制愤怒，马姆达尼的影响力不断增强
summary: Three candidates endorsed by Zohran Mamdani swept to victory in Democratic congressional primaries on Tuesday, showing that the coalition which powered the New York City mayor to a surprising victory last year is gaining strength.

title: UN-Commissioned Report Says Israel Targeted Gazan Children
title_cn: 联合国委托报告称以色列针对加沙儿童
summary: A United Nations-commissioned report has concluded that Israel deliberately targeted and killed Palestinian children during the war in Gaza, an accusation which the country rejects.

title: IAEA Says ‘War of Words’ Won’t Stop Iran Nuclear Inspections
title_cn: 国际原子能机构称“口水战”不会阻止伊朗核检查
summary: The international nuclear watchdog responsible for verifying Iran’s stockpile of near-bomb-grade uranium dismissed the conflicting signals from Tehran and Washington overnight and said it expects to resume full monitoring at some stage.

title: EU Proposes Tax Simplification Measures in Bid to Boost Business
title_cn: 欧盟提出税收简化措施以促进商业发展
summary: The European Union unveiled a new package to overhaul the bloc’s tax rules on Wednesday, the latest effort by Brussels to cut red tape and reduce the regulatory burden on business.

title: Carney Squeezes Germany and Korea as He Dangles Huge Submarine Deal
title_cn: 卡尼悬赏巨额潜艇交易，挤压德国和韩国
summary: In late May, a South Korean attack submarine slipped into a harbor in Victoria, British Columbia — a floating sales pitch for one of the largest military contracts in Canadian history.

title: South Africa Speaker Supports Ramaphosa’s Bid to Halt Impeachment
title_cn: 南非议长支持拉马福萨停止弹劾的提议
summary: South Africa’s parliamentary speaker, Thoko Didiza, will file court papers explaining the legislature’s position in President Cyril Ramaphosa’s bid to halt impeachment proceedings against him, while a parliamentary committee said it will oppose the application.

title: US and India Conclude Latest Trade Talks With No Clarity Over Deal
title_cn: 美国和印度结束最新贸易谈判，但协议未明确
summary: India’s trade minister said he had concluded talks with his US counterpart, without indicating whether the two sides had bridged the remaining gaps holding up an interim trade agreement.

title: Ukraine Says It Hit Russian Gas-Processing Plant Near Kazakhstan
title_cn: 乌克兰称袭击了哈萨克斯坦附近的俄罗斯天然气处理厂
summary: Ukrainian drones struck a gas-processing plant and a helium production facility in Russia’s Orenburg region, raising fears of a knock-on impact on oil and gas production in neighboring Kazakhstan.

title: Poland Seeks Higher Alcohol and Sugar Levies to Fund Healthcare
title_cn: 波兰寻求提高酒精和糖税来资助医疗保健
summary: Poland plans to raise 18 billion zloty ($4.8 billion) over the next decade from higher excise duty on alcohol as the government is searching for new sources of funding for the strained public healthcare system, according to the Finance Ministry.

title: Burnham to Seek ‘Flexibility’ in UK Fiscal Rules, Ally Says
title_cn: 艾莉表示，伯纳姆将寻求英国财政规则的“灵活性”
summary: A long-time ally of Britain’s next expected prime minister, Andy Burnham, said the former Manchester mayor will seek to pursue “flexibility” within the government’s spending and borrowing rules, in an indication Burnham may look to depart from the course pursued by Chancellor Rachel Reeves.

title: Rubio Meets Gulf Leaders After Rocky Start to US-Iran Talks
title_cn: 美伊会谈开局不利后卢比奥会见海湾领导人
summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met United Arab Emirates President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi on Wednesday, as he looks to assure Gulf allies of the strength of an interim peace agreement with Iran.

title: Good Vibes at US-Iran Talks Hide Big Problems
title_cn: 美伊会谈的良好气氛掩盖了大问题
summary: The thorniest issues are nowhere near to being resolved.

title: Here Are the Key Issues Dividing Trump and Senate Republicans
title_cn: 以下是特朗普和参议院共和党人之间存在分歧的关键问题
summary: Top Senate Republicans have a message for President Donald Trump: This is not working.

title: Australia’s Spy Chief Warns of Growing Terrorism, Cyber Threats
title_cn: 澳大利亚间谍头子警告恐怖主义和网络威胁日益增长
summary: Australia’s spy chief highlighted anti-semitic attacks such as the December massacre at Bondi Beach, a neo-Nazi bombing, cyber-threats by foreign governments and traditional espionage as just some of the security events his agency is trying to prevent.

title: Can India and China Finally Settle Their Himalayan Border?
title_cn: 印度和中国最终能解决喜马拉雅边界问题吗？
summary: China and India are taking steps to get relations back on track after deadly clashes along their disputed Himalayan frontier in 2020, with the two sides in May discussing how to settle their territorial disagreements.

title: Will the Supreme Court Give Trump More Control Over Regulators?
title_cn: 最高法院会给予特朗普更多对监管机构的控制权吗？
summary: For decades, certain US regulators have operated largely free of political influence as they’ve worked to keep inflation down and employment up, ensure markets are competitive and transparent, safeguard elections, and protect workers, consumers and investors. The Federal Reserve, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), the Federal Election Commission, and more than a dozen other regulatory agencies have a remit to make and enforce rules under leaders who have been protected from being remo

title: Delcy Rodríguez Is Campaigning Even Before Venezuela Calls for Vote
title_cn: 德尔西·罗德里格斯甚至在委内瑞拉呼吁投票之前就开始竞选
summary: For months, Delcy Rodríguez has been crisscrossing Venezuela atop a truck in what she describes as a pilgrimage. Motorcycles and cars follow her caravan in a scene that looks remarkably like the presidential campaign that opposition leader María Corina Machado ran less than two years ago.

title: Germany Retreats From Troubled F126 Frigate After Delays
title_cn: 德国在延误后从陷入困境的 F126 护卫舰撤退
summary: Germany said it’s shelving the $10 billion (7.6 billion pounds) purchase of six F126 anti-submarine warships, aborting what stood to be the largest procurement project in the history of the country’s navy.

title: Johannesburg Halts Some Services Because it Can’t Pay for Fuel
title_cn: 约翰内斯堡因无法支付燃油费而停止了一些服务
summary: Johannesburg, South Africa’s economic hub, has halted some essential services because it can’t pay for fuel, the main opposition party in the city said.

title: Denmark Expects US Talks on Greenland to Yield Deal by Year-End
title_cn: 丹麦预计美国格陵兰岛谈判将在年底前达成协议
summary: Denmark’s Foreign Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen expects talks with the US over Greenland to yield a deal by the end of the year, he told local newspaper Politiken.

title: China Detained Two Japanese in May on Suspicion of Smuggling
title_cn: 中国5月拘留两名涉嫌走私的日本人
summary: China detained two Japanese nationals in May on suspicion of smuggling prohibited goods, adding to ongoing diplomatic tensions between the two nations.

title: China Makes New US Warship Target for Missile Tests, Images Show
title_cn: 图片显示，中国将美国军舰作为导弹试验的新目标
summary: China has built a new US destroyer replica at a remote missile-testing site in its northwestern desert, satellite imagery shows, a target analysts say could be used to test anti-ship weapons.

title: Netherlands Lobbies US to Drop Chip Curbs Targeting ASML Sales
title_cn: 荷兰游说美国放弃针对 ASML 销售的芯片限制
summary: The Netherlands is lobbying the US to not expand export controls on semiconductor equipment, a move that would constrain ASML Holding NV’s ability to sell to China.

title: Xi Pressures Takaichi by Throttling Key Mineral Exports to Japan
title_cn: 习近平通过限制对日本的主要矿产出口向高市施压
summary: China is choking shipments of some critical minerals to Japan, a slowdown that’s hurting companies and prompting calls for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to find a diplomatic off-ramp with Beijing.

title: Berkeley Lays Out Steps to Revive UK Housing as Profit Drops 15%
title_cn: 利润下降 15% 伯克利制定复苏英国房地产业的措施
summary: Berkeley Group Holdings Plc laid out a series of measures it said the UK government should focus on to help revive homebuilding, as the ruling Labour party kicks off a process to find a successor to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

title: Burnham’s Route to Downing Street Clears as Rival Steps Aside
title_cn: 随着竞争对手的退让，伯纳姆通往唐宁街的路线畅通无阻
summary: Andy Burnham is all but certain to become the UK’s next prime minister after Darren Jones, who had been talked up as a potential rival, said he would not launch his own leadership bid.

title: Micron Earnings to Test Tech Sector After Global Selloff | Daybreak Europe 6/24/2026
title_cn: 全球抛售后，美光科技盈利将考验科技行业黎明欧洲 6/24/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

President Donald Trump said Iran will be able to use funds released from frozen accounts only to purchase food and medical supplies from the US, seeking to ease concerns about peace negotiations that both sides say are making progress.

And SpaceX sold $25 billion of investment-grade bonds, marking the final step to replace the costly debt that had helped finance Musk’s 2022 acquisition of X, then known as Twitter, as well as the expensive loans and bonds issued by artificial intelligence lab xAI last year to bridge its rapid cash drain.

Today's guest: Josie Anderson, European Economist, Nomura (Source: Bloomberg)
